# Test Fixtures

## synthetic_site.tif

**Provenance:** Procedurally generated — not derived from real-world data.
**Generated by:** `scripts/generate_test_dem.py`
**CRS:** EPSG:28354 (GDA94 / MGA Zone 54)
**Extent:** 500000–500500 E, 6100000–6100500 N (500m × 500m)
**Resolution:** 1.0 m/cell
**Size:** 500 × 500 cells

### Terrain description

The DEM contains four distinct terrain features designed to exercise all simulation components:

| Region | Rows | Cols | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| Flat plateau | 0–149 | 0–499 | 50 m elevation — clear LOS, full viewshed expected |
| Hill (NE) | 0–149 | 300–499 | Gaussian hill, peak ~120 m at (row 75, col 400) |
| Ridge | 200–249 | 0–499 | E–W ridge, peak 130 m — blocks LOS north↔south |
| Valley | 250–349 | 150–349 | Depression to 30 m — simulates terrain masking |
| Flat base | 350–499 | 0–499 | 50 m elevation — observer zone |

### Intended use

- Primary DEM for `salus viewshed` demos and integration tests
- Observer placement: row ~450, col ~250 (southern flat base), ~50 m elevation
- Expected behaviour: ridge at rows 200–249 should block most of northern terrain
- Hill at NE should create partial shadow behind it

**This file is committed to the repository.** It is small (~2 MB uncompressed, stored as
deflate-compressed GeoTIFF) and deterministic — the generator script always produces
the same output.
